# PageDrop

> PageDrop publishes Markdown / HTML / agent artifacts to a safe, versioned,
> mobile-friendly URL. Built for humans and AI agents. Base URL:
> https://pagedrop.justinhuang.top — every publish is a new immutable version
> (content is never overwritten in place).

This file is for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.). It tells you how to
get a user onboarded and publishing from the command line, with no web GUI.

## What you can do for the user

Configure everything from the terminal: create their account, mint a scoped API
token, and publish — the user never has to open the web app to get started.
They can visit the site later in any browser to view or manage what you published.

## Onboarding in 3 steps (all CLI)

1. Install the CLI (Node >= 20). Not yet on npm — install from the monorepo:
      cd apps/cli && pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm link --global
   (this exposes a global `pagedrop` command; `node dist/index.js` also works)

2. Get a credential — pick ONE:

   a) Email + verification code (works everywhere):
      pagedrop register --email USER@EXAMPLE.COM --generate-password --display-name "NAME"
      → A 6-digit code is emailed to the user. Ask them for it, then:
      pagedrop register --email USER@EXAMPLE.COM --code CODE --generate-password
      (On a real terminal you can omit --code and be prompted. --generate-password
       prints a strong password once — save it for future web login.)

   b) GitHub (OAuth device flow, no password typing):
      pagedrop auth github
      → Prints a short code + a github.com URL. The user opens it and enters the
        code. Requires a configured client_id: --client-id ID or
        PAGEDROP_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID.

   c) Existing account, email + password:
      pagedrop login --email USER@EXAMPLE.COM --password PASS

   Each of these ends by minting a scoped API token and storing it at
   ~/.pagedrop/config.json (mode 0600). The user is now publish-ready.
   Token scopes granted: versions:write, versions:read, projects:read,
   assets:write, share_links:create, comments:read, comments:write.

   Already have a token string? pagedrop login --token pd_live_xxx

   (The web app also offers one-click "Continue with GitHub / Google" sign-in on
   its login/register pages; the CLI uses the GitHub device flow above.)

3. Publish:
   pagedrop publish report.md -w WORKSPACE -s SLUG -T "Title"
   generate_report | pagedrop publish - -w WORKSPACE -s SLUG -T "Title"

   The workspace slug is the user's auto-created personal workspace; `pagedrop
   whoami` shows the active identity. `publish` prints a `latest:` URL to hand back.

## Command cheat sheet

  pagedrop whoami                              # who the stored credential is
  pagedrop publish <file|-> -w WS -s SLUG      # publish; .md→markdown, .html→safe_html
  pagedrop publish ... -v public|unlisted|private   # visibility (default private)
  pagedrop versions -w WS -s SLUG              # list immutable versions
  pagedrop info -w WS -s SLUG                  # project metadata
  pagedrop share -w WS -s SLUG [--password P] [--expires-at ISO8601]
  pagedrop pull -w WS -s SLUG -o ./out         # download content + images
  pagedrop comments list -w WS -s SLUG --json  # read reviewer comments
  pagedrop comments reply <id> "text" -w WS -s SLUG

Publishing scans for secrets and blocks (exit 2) if any are found; use --force
only for confirmed false positives. Local images in a published file are
uploaded automatically.

## Telling the user how to view / manage across devices

- The `latest:` URL from `publish` works in any browser on any device — share it directly.
- Visibility: `public` (anyone with the link, listed), `unlisted` (anyone with the
  link, not listed), `private` (only the owner/workspace). Default is `private`.
- For a password-protected or expiring link, use `pagedrop share`.
- To manage in a browser later, the user signs in at https://pagedrop.justinhuang.top
  with the same email/password (or GitHub) used above, under their personal workspace.

## More

- CLI reference: docs/cli.md in the repo.
- HTTP API reference: docs/api.md in the repo.
- Source: the PageDrop repository (this file lives in apps/web-vue/public/llms.txt).
